name: Outemu Optical Switch
summary: Budget-friendly full-size optical switch — cheapest optical option, used in many affordable optical keyboards
pinDescription: No electrical pins — PCB-mounted IR emitter/phototransistor. Switch has alignment posts and light channel only.
hotswapNote: Proprietary Outemu optical hot-swap design. NOT compatible with Gateron optical PCBs despite similar principles.
scanningNote: Same PCB-mounted IR design concept as Gateron optical, but proprietary PCB layout. NOT cross-compatible.
footprintNote: No official KiCad footprint. Would need reverse-engineering from Tecware/Redragon optical PCBs (which use Outemu optical).
designNotes: Among the cheapest optical switches available (~$0.18/switch).
Used in many budget optical keyboards: Tecware Phantom, Redragon K556 Optical, etc.
Same PCB-level design complexity as Gateron optical — requires IR pairs per key position.
Outemu optical PCB layouts are NOT cross-compatible with Gateron optical layouts.
No low-profile variant available from Outemu.
features: Standard MX stem — compatible with all MX keycaps
concerns: Same PCB-mounted optical component complexity as Gateron optical
Hot-swap sockets NOT interchangeable with Gateron or any other optical system
Full MX height only — no low-profile option
Lower build quality perception vs Gateron optical
